Find out more about this, as well as the new capability to upload and download attachments from tasks in this blog post:

  • SAP Mobile Start V1.12 - Release Update

October 16th update: SAP Mobile Start V2.0 is now available, supporting Spaces & Pages to group your applications; cards from SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition; Situation Handling standard framework capabilities; and enhancements to task handling as well as optimized layouts for larger screens, as provided by foldables and tablets on Android. Check them out in this blog post:

  • SAP Mobile Start V2.0 - Release Update

News for UI Developers, Web and Mobile

The major news for UI developers for both web and mobile is that SAP Build now supports ABAP Cloud, and not just CAP (Cloud Application Programming model) for other programming languages such as Java and JavaScript. Get a comprehensive overview in this blog post from Michael Ameling:

  • Unveiling today: SAP Build meets ABAP Cloud!

Providing Joule skills is also a powerful way to enhance the user experience – I covered our main planned innovations there in already in the “Planned Innovations” part of the above Joule section.

News for Web Developers

Alt Text: Image of the white UI5 logo on a blue background with the text “UI5 linter 1.0 New Release” below it.

The main news is that UI5 linter 1.0 has now been released and is generally available. It is a static code analysis tool which helps ensure that you have good quality code, and which saves you a lot of time debugging later by catching issues up front. It can check for problems such as the use of deprecated UI5 libraries, framework APIs etc. as well as the usage of global variables and possible CSP violations. Find out more in this blot post:

  • Release of UI5 linter v1.0

I would also like to point you to the updated SAP BTP Developer’s Guide, where we have now brought in user experience design advice, as well as restructuring it to cover Explore, Discover, Design, Deliver and Scale & Run phases:

  • SAP BTP Developer’s Guide
  • If you read the guide, do spend a few minutes to give us feedback, so that we can improve it even further, by taking this survey to help us improve the guide!

The developer’s guide recommends using SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori elements, built on OData services, for developing UIs. Should you not be able to follow this recommendation because you have existing applications using different protocols or frameworks, we have options to help you nevertheless:

  • If you have existing back-end code which cannot provide OData services, we have now released the SAPUI5 meta-data controls library sap.ui.mdc, as outlined here:
    -  Smart choices for your UI: When to use sap.ui.mdc
  • If you already have applications written with a different UI technology, such as Angular or React, we have released Version 2.0 of our UI5 Web Components:
    -  Blast Off! UI5 Web Components 2.0 is Now Live!

For friends of ABAP, we recently introduced the ability to create Fiori elements apps directly from within the ABAP Development Tools (ADT). Find out more here:

  • How to create a SAP Fiori Elements application from within ADT?
  • SAP Fiori tools May 2024 release adds closer integration with ABAP Development Tools

News for Mobile Developers

The main news for mobile developers is the latest releases of the mobile SDKs, as well as the integration of the Mobile Development Kit into SAP Build Code. Here are links to find out more:

  • How to Develop SAP MDK Applications in SAP Build Code
  • Streamline SAP MDK Development with the Power of Generative AI and Joule
  • SAP BTP SDK for iOS 24.8.0 is now available
  • SAP BTP SDK for Android 24.8.0 is now available

For a deeper dive, watch the recordings of the Devtoberfest sessions on developing mobile apps:

  • Cross-Platform Mobile App Development with SAP Build Code and the MDK (50:40 minutes)
  • Craft Enterprise Apps for iOS, iPadOS, and visionOS with SAP Fiori and SAP Build Code (47:16 minutes)
  • Build Elegant Enterprise Apps for Android with SAP Fiori and SAP Build Code (45:24 minutes)

Final Words

I hope you are as excited about these new user experience innovations as I am! FPS02 brings quite a few additional new features beyond user experience, discover all of them here:

  • What’s New Viewer for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS02

If you like what you see here, we encourage you to upgrade to FPS02, so that you can run your business even better with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023.

entry.title: Revolutionized What's New Viewer Experience in SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
We have made an exciting new change in our What’s New Viewer for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. The viewer now lets you compare new features and changes between SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, and check which are available in both.

This is made possible because both products share a single What’s New Viewer. Your default scope selection will depend on where you access the What’s New Viewer from; either the SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition product pages on SAP Help Portal.

You can change the scope by using the options in the “Availability” filter. By selecting SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition you will view the features and changes that are available for both products. Selecting either SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition will filter to features and changes only available for that individual product.

We hope this update will give a broader understanding to both products, and support your daily What’s New Viewer experience.

entry.title: Designing with Code - Update to Version 2 of UI5 Web Components
entry.content:
Designing with Code – Why Should I?

In my article series Designing with Code, I shared my experience of spending six months working within a product design team. By stepping out of my comfort zone—my beloved design tool—and diving into creating first prototypes using UI5 web components, I found myself much more connected to the actual end result. Using my entry-level coding skills, I gained insights into what happens behind the pixels, which greatly improved my discussions with developers.

In subsequent articles, I introduced how designers can leverage web components, CSS, JavaScript, and HTML to create interactive prototypes. This hands-on approach not only deepens your understanding of components but also allows you to experience how they behave in real-world scenarios.

To clarify: I am not a front-end developer. But just as a basic understanding of design benefits developers, designers can work more efficiently by grasping the basics of coding. This is particularly helpful when exploring how components interact or how screen readers interpret your designs. For me, it was a true eye-opener.

I hope this inspires you to get started! You might want to revisit Part 3, where I cover a simple prototyping setup, or Part 4, which introduces the model-view-controller pattern.

An Update: Why Some Examples No Longer Work

Unfortunately, the examples in my previous articles are now outdated due to two major changes:

  • Updated UI5 Documentation: The UI5 team migrated their documentation to a new system, integrating a sleek playground feature. This allows you to view live example code, make modifications, and preview results directly in the browser. If you haven’t tried it yet, I highly recommend it! However, this migration rendered the old links to the UI5 web components library invalid, breaking the examples in my articles.
  • Release of UI5 Web Components Version 2: A new version of UI5 web components introduced several improvements, including refactoring and stabilization, as well as exciting new components.

Given these changes, I’ve updated the examples in the GitHub repository to align with the latest version:

GitHub Repository – Designing with Code

Disclaimer: The prototyping setup described here is not intended for production use. For building production-grade applications, please refer to the official UI5 documentation.

Simple Prototyping Setup

Building production-ready applications often involves complex tools like build systems, testing frameworks, and dependency management. For designers, this can be overwhelming and time-consuming. Thankfully, there are simpler alternatives for prototyping:

Playground

The UI5 playground allows you to experiment with components by editing code and seeing instant results. This is sufficient for most exploratory tasks. However, the playground doesn’t allow for permanent saving or advanced interactions.

Local Prototyping Setup

For more complex prototypes or continuous work, a local setup offers greater flexibility. Below, I outline a minimal installation and configuration process that avoids heavy tools while providing key advantages:

  • No need to manage tools or packages locally.
  • No build steps—simply refresh your browser.
  • Easy sharing of prototypes.

Setting Up the Prototype: Key Changes in the New Approach

Import Map vs. Bundle

Previously, we imported a single bundle that included all libraries, making every component available but at a performance cost. The new approach uses an import map, which loads only the components you explicitly reference, improving efficiency and compatibility with real deployments.

To include a component, locate the <script> tag in the UI5 playground under the expanded code header. Copy and paste it into your HTML file.

I recommend using the sources available on the GitHub repository to get started. These files might also serve you as basis for your own projects going forward. The previous examples are still available. I have added an updated version in parallel folders:

  • Old Setup: Part-03-SAP-Web-Components/01_button
  • New Setup: Part-03-SAP-Web-Components 2.0/01_button

Manual Imports

With the new setup, you must manually import each required component. Create a main.js file in the same folder as your index.html and add the relevant imports. For example, to use a button component:

 

 

import "@ui5/webcomponents/dist/Button.js";

 

 

If a component doesn’t render as expected, double-check your imports.

The import statement, that is needed to import a component can be found in the documentation under the section “ES6 Module Import”. Figure 3 shows the import statement in the documentation for the Avatar component.

Running the Prototype: HTTP Server Required

Modern browsers require HTTP servers to dynamically load resources. You can use:

Visual Studio Code with Live Preview

Install the Live Preview extension for an integrated HTTP server in Visual Studio Code.

(Recommended for simplicity)

Node.js and http-server

http-server is a lightweight alternative for more control over your setup.

Is It Worth the Effort?

Admittedly, this new setup requires more effort compared to the old bundle-based approach. However, it offers significant advantages:

  1. Lightweight and Portable: Avoid managing local installations while benefiting from version control. Share your prototypes easily using platforms like GitHub Pages.
  2. Realistic Testing: Standalone prototypes allow you to test interactions like keyboard behavior, screen reader support, and resizing without playground constraints.
  3. Developer-Ready Code: The new setup aligns more closely with production code, enabling developers to reuse your prototypes effectively.

For quick experiments, the playground remains the fastest option. But for long-term projects or detailed explorations, this setup strikes a great balance between simplicity and functionality.

entry.title: My Home in SAP S/4HANA - A Deep Dive as of 2408
entry.content:
My Home is your personalized entry page within SAP S/4HANA,  that provides you with easy access to the business insights, tasks, and applications you need to do your job with greater speed and agility. My Home lets you access and manage your to-dos, your favorite pages, your favorite apps, and your insights on any device. It builds on the functionality of the SAP Fiori launchpad and leverages Spaces and Pages (as a prerequisite). The new My Home was first released with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2208 release. Since its first release, there have been great improvements in its functionality and user experience. In this blog, we’ll revisit the features of My Home that will enhance your user experience and productivity.  

Video: Welcome to the My Home in SAP S/4HANA 

My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

 

 

My Home Sections 

 

The My Home entry page is divided into different sections. You can personalize the content of each section to fit your specific needs. 

 

To-Dos 

The To-Dos section provides an overview of your daily tasks. It combines your tasks from the My Inbox application and your situations from the standard and extended My Situations applications. The tasks display relevant business information about your assigned workflow items. It enables you to process your tasks without ever having to leave My Home. If Collaborative ERP is enabled on the system, you can collaborate directly with colleagues via MS Teams to resolve issues about your tasks quickly.  

Video: Managing the To-Dos Section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home To Do Section

 

 

 

News 

The News section keeps you updated on your company's latest news, or SAP S/4HANA system news. If SAP S/4HANA system news is displaying, you can shuffle through system news from different lines of business. You can manage SAP S/4HANA system news by choosing a line of business  that interests you to stay focused on relevant information. 

Video: Personalizing the News section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home News Section

 

 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home System News articles

 

 

 

Pages  

The Pages section provides you with quick access to your favorite or most used pages. You can manage the visibility of your pages and order them in a way that enhances your productivity.  

Video: Personalizing the Pages section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home Pages Section

 

 

 

Apps 

The Apps section provides easy access to your favorite, most used, recently used, and recommended applications. The Apps section improves the discoverability of your applications. The Recommended tab offers app suggestions using SAP Business AI, based on your user-specific role assignment. You can add apps to the Favorites tab from your most used, recently used, and recommended applications. You can organize and categorize your favorite applications by using the grouping, ordering and color-coding functionality so you can access them even more efficiently. 

Video: Personalizing the Apps section 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home Apps Section

 

 

 

Insights Tiles and Insights Cards 

The Insights section allows you to manage and monitor your company’s activities. It shows critical business information through KPIs and micro charts. This section is broken into two sections: Insights Tiles and Insights Cards. Insights cards and tiles display dynamic content at a glance. They provide quick and easy access to the applications that can provide more detailed information. Insights tiles offer analytical information, such as charts or KPIs. Insights cards are similar to Insights tiles but provide more detailed analytical information in the form of charts, tables or lists from supporting overview page-, list report-, and analytical list page-based applications. Thanks to this information, you can assess what is happening at any time and take action when needed.  You can customize the Insights section to display information that enables you to do your job with speed and agility. 

Video: Personalizing the Insights Tiles Section 

Video: Personalizing the Insights Cards Section 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home Insights Section

 

 

The My Home Layout and Advanced Settings 

 You can personalize the individual My Home sections, and another key feature is the ability to customize the layout of these sections on My Home. This personalization capability allows you to prioritize the information that is most important to you. You can manage the visibility and the display order of each of the My Home sections. 

Video: Personalizing the Layout of My Home  

Once you have personalized the My Home sections you can easily share your personalized content with co-workers who have the same or a similar role as you. This  enables them to also work with speed and agility. 

Video: Sharing the Personalized Content 

 

 

 

Now that we have given you a recap of the available My Home functionality in SAP S/4HANA, we hope you enjoy working with My Home in SAP S/4HANA and reap its benefits. You can  look forward to more enhancements that will improve your My Home experience even further in future releases of SAP S/4HANA.  

Note: Innovations appear first in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The demos in the videos were recorded on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in September 2024. 

Stay tuned! 

Caroline Welsh and Anna Freitag 

Product Managers of My Home, Cloud ERP UX Engineering.

entry.title: Design with Accessibility in Mind: SAP’s Accessibility Design Tools
entry.content:
Building products with accessibility in mind is vital to creating great user experiences. At SAP, we believe accessibility should be part of the design process from the beginning. When accessibility is considered early on, creating products that work for everyone is easier, leading to better results for users and teams.

To support designers and developers in this effort, we’ve created a set of Accessibility Design Tools. These tools make it easier to design with accessibility in mind, helping you build more inclusive products while saving time and effort along the way. Whether you’re a designer, product manager, or developer, our tools are here to help you create products that are accessible from the start.

Blue background banner with accessibility symbol representing a human figure with arms raised on the right. Symbol is hovering over a wireframe user interface mockup and sends three rays to different interface positions. Contains text “Accessibility Design Tools - Plugins and Accessibility Annotations for Designers."

The Accessibility Design Tools Handbook

One way to create products with improved accessibility is by embedding accessibility into the design process. When accessibility is considered early, teams can build more efficient workflows during the design phase, leading to a more streamlined development process.

That’s where SAP’s Accessibility Design Tools Handbook comes in. This guide empowers designers, developers, and product managers to integrate accessibility right from the start. It provides practical steps for annotating designs to meet accessibility standards, including those from the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG), while encouraging broader considerations throughout the design process. With this resource, teams can ensure their products are designed with accessibility in mind.

The Handbook helps make accessibility a natural part of the design process, leading to more efficient development and better user experiences for all.

Infographic of various accessibility annotation elements, such as wayfinding, tooltips, error prevention, and speech output. The elements are displayed in a variety of colors and shapes.

Annotation Library

Manually annotating designs for accessibility can be time-consuming and tedious and lead to inconsistencies. To help streamline this process, we collaborated with teams across SAP to develop the Annotation Library, which provides designers with a set of annotations covering about 70% of the WCAG 2.2 success criteria. The annotations are organized across five core areas:

  • Foundation: Helps designers prepare UI components for accessible interaction and navigation, covering input and output mechanisms, consistency, and reusable component names to ensure an efficient user experience
  • Visual Design: Focuses on color contrast, themes, and sensory concepts, while ensuring UI elements behave correctly during text resizing, zooming, orientation changes, and contrast adjustments.
  • Screen Reader: Guides designers in structuring reading order, headings, and landmarks with annotations to create an intuitive experience beyond what’s visible on the screen.
  • Keyboard Support: Provides annotation for preparing screens and components for keyboard navigation, including focus order, shortcuts, and identifying initial focus and skipping groups.
  • Complementary Assets: Offers annotations for additional considerations, such as multimedia, time limits, and error prevention, to enhance accessibility across different user needs.

Figma Plugin

Annotating multiple screens and every UI element can be time-consuming. To reduce the time burden and increase efficiency, we created a Figma Plugin based on a subset of annotation assets, including:

  • Screen reading order
  • Focus order
  • Landmarks
  • Headings

The plugin is a free beta version and can be accessed on Figma Community.

Ready to Design Products with Accessibility in Mind?

By using the Annotation Library and Figma Plugin together, teams can streamline the process of integrating accessibility into their designs. These tools not only make it easier to meet accessibility standards like WCAG 2.2 but also empower designers to create more inclusive products from the start.

With these resources, accessibility becomes a natural part of the design process, leading to better user experiences and more efficient collaboration and workflows for teams.

Make accessibility an integral part of your design process:

  • Download the Accessibility Design Tools Handbook
  • Access the Annotation Library in the Figma Community
  • Try out the Accessibility Design Tools Plugin for Figma
  • Explore the Accessibility Design Tools Plugin Code available open source on GitHub

entry.title: SAP Screen Personas 3.0 SP19 enhances usability, adds customization options, streamlines workflows
entry.content:
During our monthly SAP Screen Personas practitioner forum meetings, we listen closely to your feedback and insights. As always, customer requests guide our innovations in SAP Screen Personas, ensuring the tool evolves to meet your specific needs. With SP19, we're thrilled to introduce a set of new features designed to further enhance usability, streamline customization, and support efficient workflows.  

Here’s what’s new in SP19!

  • Use multiple table columns to set up conditional formatting.
  • A new option to disable the software keyboard for the relevant input fields.
  • A new option in the Label control and Button controls that allows you to adjust the icon size. (Available only in the Slipstream Engine)
  • You can now introduce toast messages in scripting  (Available only in the Slipstream Engine)

As a reminder, SAP Screen Personas support now runs for two years for each service pack. So, SP19 will remain in support through November 2026. We are supporting SAP Screen Personas functionality in SAP S/4HANA through at least 2040. If you are on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP Screen Personas capabilities are embedded as Adapt UI for Classic Apps; nothing to install; it’s ready for use.

Use multiple table columns to set up conditional formatting.

Conditional formatting allows you to highlight a row, based on its contents. We created this feature in an early version of SAP Screen Personas in response to a customer request to highlight line items that contained radioactive components as they were subject to additional regulations. Customers also use this for more mundane uses to flag rows that exceed certain values or contain specific text.

With SP19, we add the ability to compare two columns to set the formatting for a row, based on a condition. For example, if the value in Column1 is “Greater Than” the value in Column2, highlight the row in red.

To include a second column in conditional formatting, you use the same rule creation window. The last column now provides a dropdown menu to select a column for comparison, or you can enter a “Comparison value” as before.

A new option to disable the software keyboard for the relevant input fields.

This feature is designed to enhance user experience in applications that rely on automated input methods, such as scanner devices, barcode readers, and RFID readers. The Advanced Property Editor now includes a new Boolean property, hideSoftwareKeyboard, for GuiTextField and GuiCTextField control types. By setting hideSoftwareKeyboard to TRUE, you can prevent the on-screen keyboard from appearing when these fields are selected. This avoids unnecessary interruptions in workflows where physical or automated data entry is used, eliminating the need for the software keyboard.

This property is especially beneficial in high-efficiency environments such as retail, warehousing, and manufacturing, where input fields are populated through scanning instead of manual typing. By minimizing unnecessary distractions and reducing screen clutter, hideSoftwareKeyboard streamlines operations, resulting in a faster and more seamless data entry process. Additionally, it helps maintain a cleaner interface, allowing operators to focus on tasks without extra steps to dismiss the keyboard, ultimately supporting a more efficient and user-friendly workflow.

A new option in the Label control and Button controls that allows you to adjust the icon size. (Available only in the Slipstream Engine)

A new property called iconFontSize is now available forLabel and Button controls having only icon. This editable property enables you to customize the icon's display size directly within the label component, providing greater control over the icon's visual prominence and alignment. By you can enhance readability, emphasize important elements, or ensure consistency across different screen sizes and resolutions. This update is particularly useful for creating more polished and user-friendly interfaces, allowing for finer adjustments that align with the overall design aesthetic.

 

 

You can now introduce toast messages in scripting (Available only in the Slipstream Engine)

A toast message is a small, non-disruptive popup message that gives the user some information and then disappears automatically after a few seconds. In SP19, a new scripting API command has been introduced for displaying non-interactive popup messages similar to the "messageToast" in SAPUI5. Previously, there was no equivalent API in the Screen Personas script editor for creating toast notifications that don’t require user interaction. With this new command, you can now easily implement message toasts for simple scenarios, allowing for streamlined feedback that aligns with SAP Fiori standards. This addition helps enhance user experience by providing subtle, non-disruptive messages in SAP Screen Personas.

Example:  session.utils.showMessageToast("This is a toast message");

SP19 related notes:

  • SP19 release note contains all the kernel pre-requisites: https://me.sap.com/notes/3525171
  •  SP19 client note:  https://me.sap.com/notes/3525180

Your feedback matters!

These updates with SP19 reflect our commitment to helping you use SAP Screen Personas to simplify classic apps to suit the unique demands of your operations, regardless of industry or role. By adding new functionality, improving customization options, and ensuring stability, SP19 enables you to build a more efficient, user-centric SAP experience. We’re excited to see how these enhancements will support your ongoing success and look forward to continuing to bring your ideas to life.

Thank you for your feedback and participation in our forums your insights are invaluable as we strive to make SAP Screen Personas even more powerful and responsive to your needs.

You already own SAP Screen Personas
We still receive questions about whether there’s an additional license fee for SAP Screen Personas. There’s no extra charge—SAP Screen Personas is already included with your ERP license. Thousands of customers are using SAP Screen Personas to streamline screens in their classic apps on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP S/4HANA, and ECC. Changes made with SAP Screen Personas are upgrade-safe, allowing you to build flavors while keeping a clean core.

Learn more about SAP Screen Personas

  • Online training. The best way to learn the basics about SAP Screen Personas (Adapt UI for classic applications) is to take our free training course on the learning.sap.com platform: https://learning.sap.com/courses/adapting-the-ui-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-using-sap-screen-personas
  • Connect with your peers. Every other month, we have a group of customers that gather online to share best practices, demo their flavors, and answer each other’s questions. Experts from the product team also join to explain how to use the latest functionality. If you are interested in joining, you can sign up online to join the SAP Screen Personas practitioner forum. This will add you to the mailing list for the monthly meeting summaries. Please contact “Peter Spielvogel“ or me directly if you want to join the monthly calls.
  • Product documentation. The SAP Screen Personas documentation, recently updated to be even more clear and comprehensive, contains the latest information about the product.

Going forward with SAP Screen Personas

  1. If you are on SP17 or earlier, we recommend you upgrade now, ideally going directly to SP19.
  2. If you are not currently using SAP Screen Personas, why not?
    Worried that we won’t support it into the future? Don’t be.  We are supporting SAP Screen Personas functionality through at least 2040.
    Have other concerns about the roadmap for SAP Screen Personas? See if we answer them in our latest roadmap blog post.the ability to adapt the UI of classic apps is something important to us.
    Have other concerns about the roadmap for SAP Screen Personas? See if we answer them in our latest roadmap blog post.

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Updated Transport to Production Strategy for Rapid Activation
entry.content:
If you are operating with SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA, you will need to transport your launchpad content, layout, and other configuration settings from your Development to your Production environment. You can now find an updated document with the latest advice on the transport strategy for SAP Fiori Rapid Activation and related SAP Fiori objects at

SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Rapid Activation Transport Move to Production Strategy

 

What is SAP Fiori Rapid Activation?

As a reminder, the SAP Fiori Rapid Activation procedure lets you, as a system administrator, quickly activate the capabilities of the SAP Fiori launchpad and SAP Fiori applications using a series of task lists.

Refer to

  • As explained in the central note for all SAP S/4HANA releases, SAP Note 2902673 - Rapid Activation for SAP Fiori in SAP S/4HANA - Overview
  • SAP Help - SAP Fiori Overview - Rapid Activation of SAP Fiori
  • SAP Blog - SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Activate SAP Fiori in 1 day or less - the video playlist and FAQ
  • SAP Blog - SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Creating your custom business roles – the end-to-end process and video playlist

What’s changed in this version?

This document has the latest information for SAP S/4HANA 2023 release including

  • Considerations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customers
  • Clearer explanation of how transport of Rapid Activation works
  • Prerequisites for transporting Rapid Activation
  • Which task lists to run on implementation versus upgrade
  • Mandatory/Optional tasks to run in Development versus subsequent systems
  • Critical parameters for transports
  • Coverage of new task list SAP_FIORI_FCM_CATALOG_ACTIVATION
  • How to transport settings in other Rapid Activation tools such as Launchpad configuration parameters in transaction /UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF

For official guidance for your SAP S/4HANA release and FPS, always refer to the Composite SAP note: Rapid Activation for SAP Fiori in SAP S/4HANA <release> <FPSxx>" for your SAP S/4HANA release and FPS (example: SAP Note 3469488 - Composite SAP note: Rapid Activation for SAP Fiori in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02)

The links to these SAP notes are available in the document.

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru


You’ll find much more on the community topic page for SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Other helpful links in the SAP Community:

  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA


Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – [Updated] Moving from Standalone to Embedded Deployment in SAP Fiori
entry.content:
As you may already know, SAP recommends deploying SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA with your SAP Fiori Front-End Server in embedded mode (embedded FES) , as outlined in the SAP Fiori Deployment Options and System Landscape Recommendations. 

Embedded FES reduces server cost and complexity and simplifies ongoing operations as you grow your usage of SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA innovations.

If you began your SAP S/4HANA journey with a SAP Fiori Front-End Server in standalone mode (standalone FES), you might be asking:
How do I transition from standalone FES to embedded FES deployment?

The Regional Implementation Group for SAP S/4HANA has worked with many customers to help them move to SAP Fiori Front-End Server Embedded deployment. Based on these experiences, we have created the first version of the document in 2019 as an easy-to-follow, step-by-step guide. This guide outlined the migration process, explaining both the technical setup and how to ensure a smooth experience for users.

We’re excited to announce our updated Moving from Standalone to Embedded Deployment in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guide based on SAP S/4HANA release 2023.

This new version provides more streamlined steps, updated tools and further recommendations to help you successfully move from standalone FES to embedded FES deployment.

Moving from Standalone to Embedded Deployment in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Why do you need this guide?

This guide is essential for SAP S/4HANA customers who are looking to move from a standalone FES to an embedded FES deployment. It provides clear, step-by-step instructions to make the transition smooth, covering both technical and practical aspects of the migration.

Whether you are upgrading your SAP S/4HANA system or aligning with SAP's latest architectural recommendations, this guide ensures you have everything you need i.e. from planning, installation, and configuration to post-migration testing and optimization. It helps you:

  • Understand the benefits of moving to embedded FES deployment.
  • Know the right time to make the shift.
  • Navigate technical prerequisites, sizing, and system considerations.
  • Simplify the migration process with actionable steps, tools, and examples.
  • Ensure a seamless experience for users by resolving common issues.
  • Minimize disruptions by retaining user personalization and role assignments.
  • Gain tips on how to enhance usability and performance

By following this guide, you can align with SAP best practices to unlock the full potential of SAP Fiori embedded FES deployment.

 

What's in it / What's new?

  • Enhance performance with tips for optimizing launchpad features, OData services, and app configurations

    • This includes reviewing and improving performance for SAP GUI Apps, SAP Fiori Apps, SAP Fiori Launchpad etc. and best practices to adjust and transport launchpad configuration parameters
  • Why and how to use Clean Core SAP Fiori administration tools to perform the migration
    • Clean core tools like Launchpad Content Manager, Launchpad Application Manager, Manage Launchpad Pages, Manage Launchpad Spaces provide enhanced capabilities compared to classical tools like Launchpad Designer. 
  • Structured 6-Step Content Migration
    • Comprehensive steps to migrate roles, catalogs, spaces and pages.
  • User Personalization Retention
    • Tools like /UIF/MIGRATE_FES_PERSO to migrate homepages, private views, and UI changes.
  • Multi-Client Activation
    • Detailed guidance on enabling and configuring new clients post-migration.
  • Improved Testing and Troubleshooting
    • Expanded scenarios for UAT, error handling using App Support, and validation tools.
  • Performance and usability enhancements
    • Parameters for faster navigation, optimized search, and stateful SAP GUI containers.
  • Web Dispatcher Guidance
    • Steps to switch from Standalone to Embedded without changing end-user URLs.

 

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Other helpful links:

  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series Starts Next Week!
entry.content:
GROW with SAP is a comprehensive package combining solutions, services, community support, and learnings to help businesses of all sizes successfully adopt cloud ERP. At its core lies SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, our fully-fledged cloud ERP designed to optimize processes and drive intelligent, sustainable operations. 

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Early Release Series is a biannual event showcasing live sessions led by product experts. These sessions provide an exclusive preview of upcoming innovations, including AI-driven features and advancements with Joule, tailored to selected lines of business and industries. Spanning several days, the series offers expert demos, insider previews, and real-time Q&A, giving participants a first look at exciting updates and the opportunity to get their questions answered directly.

From January 21 to January 24, 2025, we'll start the year with 21 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.

 

Register here:

Product ComplianceClick me!
Asset ManagementClick me!
SAP Activate, SAP Cloud ALM, Test Automation ToolClick me!

 

Sign-Up Instructions:

An active SAP Learning Hub license is required to register for the live sessions and to consume the recordings. Find out how to sign up​

  • As a customer:​ Access to at least the SAP Enterprise Support Edition is required. Follow these steps to sign up:​
    1. Navigate to the sign-up page and click on the “sign up” button.​
    2. Authenticate yourself with your S-user ID and password.​
    3. The system will check your eligibility, create a new SAP Learning Hub user, and populate your learning catalogue. This process may take up to 2 hours.​
    4. You will receive a registration confirmation via e-mail once the registration process is complete.​
  • As a partner: Partners can access the live sessions and recordings through the SAP Partner Edge Edition.​
    ​
  • As an SAP internal: Register here for the SAP Learning Hub Internal Edition (free of charge) by clicking on "access".​

Experiencing issues? Make sure to be logged on before registering. In case of any other issues, reach out to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Live Product Expert Sessions.

Find out more about our live product expert sessions here.
